title: Semantic Search

story.i_want_to: search knowledge articles by semantic similarity and combined keyword+semantic modes
story.so_that: I can find conceptually related articles even when exact keywords don't appear in the text

rationale: Keyword search requires exact term matches, which fails when agents describe problems differently than article authors wrote about solutions. Semantic search uses vector embeddings to find conceptually similar content regardless of vocabulary. The combined mode merges both approaches with configurable weighting, giving the best of both worlds: precision from keyword matching and recall from semantic similarity. Falling back to keyword-only when embeddings are unavailable ensures the system degrades gracefully rather than failing.

acceptance_criteria.description: `Knowledge.search_semantic/3` accepts `(tenant_id, query_embedding, opts)` where query_embedding is a float list. Returns top-K results ordered by cosine similarity (ascending distance via `<=>` operator). Each result includes a `similarity_score` (1 - cosine_distance).
Semantic search only considers articles with non-null embeddings. Articles without embeddings are excluded from results.
Results are filterable via opts: `:project_id`, `:category`, `:tags`, `:status` (default: `:published`). Same filter behavior as keyword search.

`Knowledge.search_combined/3` accepts `(tenant_id, query_string, opts)` and runs both keyword search and semantic search. Opts include `:keyword_weight` (default 0.5) and `:semantic_weight` (default 0.5) which must sum to 1.0.

Combined search generates the query embedding on-the-fly by calling `@embedding_client.generate_embedding/1` with the query string. If embedding generation fails, falls back to keyword-only search and includes a `fallback: true` flag in the response meta.

Combined search merges results by normalizing scores from each search to 0-1 range, then computing `final_score = keyword_weight * normalized_keyword_score + semantic_weight * normalized_semantic_score`. When all scores in a result set are equal (max == min), normalize all to 1.0 to avoid division by zero. Duplicate articles (present in both result sets) are merged, keeping the higher combined score.
Combined search deduplicates results by article ID. The final result set is sorted by `final_score` descending and paginated.
All search functions are scoped to the current tenant via both application-level WHERE clause and RLS policy.
If keyword_weight + semantic_weight do not sum to 1.0, the function returns `{:error, :invalid_weights}`.

test_cases.name: semantic search returns results ordered by cosine similarity
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, status: :published}) as article_a with embedding close to query_vector
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, status: :published}) as article_b with embedding far from query_vector
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, status: :published}) as article_c with no embedding (nil)
expected_results: article_a appears first (highest similarity_score)
article_b appears second (lower similarity_score)
article_c is excluded (no embedding)
Each result has a similarity_score between 0 and 1

test_cases.name: combined search merges keyword and semantic results with deduplication
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Error Handling', body: 'Try rescue patterns...', status: :published}) with embedding
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Fault Tolerance', body: 'Supervisor trees prevent cascading...', status: :published}) with embedding semantically close to 'error handling'
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Deployment', body: 'Error-free deploys...', status: :published}) with unrelated embedding
MockEmbeddingClient returns embedding for query 'error handling'
expected_results: 'Error Handling' article appears (matched by both keyword and semantic)
'Fault Tolerance' article appears (matched by semantic similarity)
'Deployment' article may appear with lower score (keyword match on 'error')
No duplicate article IDs in results
Results ordered by final_score descending

test_cases.name: combined search falls back to keyword-only on embedding failure
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Testing Guide', body: 'Unit test patterns...', status: :published})
MockEmbeddingClient expects generate_embedding/1 and returns {:error, :service_unavailable}
expected_results: Returns {:ok, result} with results from keyword search only
result.meta includes fallback: true
The 'Testing Guide' article is found via keyword match

test_cases.name: combined search rejects invalid weights
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
expected_results: Returns {:error, :invalid_weights}
No search is executed

test_cases.name: semantic search filters by project_id and category
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:project, %{tenant_id: tenant.id}) as project
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: project.id, category: :pattern, status: :published}) with embedding
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, project_id: nil, category: :reference, status: :published}) with embedding
expected_results: project_id filter returns only the project-scoped article
category filter returns only the :pattern article

test_cases.name: tenant isolation in semantic search
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant_a
fixture(:tenant) as tenant_b
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant_a.id, status: :published}) with embedding
expected_results: Returns empty results
Tenant A's article is not visible to tenant B

test_cases.name: combined search with custom weights
preconditions: fixture(:tenant) as tenant
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Exact Match', body: 'deployment pipeline...', status: :published}) with low semantic similarity
fixture(:article, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, title: 'Conceptual Match', body: 'CI/CD workflow...', status: :published}) with high semantic similarity to 'deployment'
MockEmbeddingClient returns embedding for query 'deployment'
expected_results: With semantic_weight=0.8, 'Conceptual Match' ranks higher
With keyword_weight=0.8, 'Exact Match' ranks higher
Weight configuration meaningfully affects ranking order

technical_notes: Cosine distance query: `from a in Article, where: not is_nil(a.embedding), order_by: fragment("embedding <=> ?", ^query_embedding), select_merge: %{similarity_score: fragment("1 - (embedding <=> ?)", ^query_embedding)}`.

Score normalization for combined search: `normalized = (score - min_score) / (max_score - min_score)`. When all scores in a result set are equal (max == min), normalize all to 1.0 to avoid division by zero.
Deduplication: build a map keyed by article_id, merge entries keeping the higher combined score, then sort and paginate the merged list.

Run keyword search in parallel with embedding generation using Task.Supervisor.async_nolink, then run semantic search once embedding is available. Pattern: keyword_task = Task.Supervisor.async_nolink(Loopctl.TaskSupervisor, fn -> search_keyword(...) end); {:ok, embedding} = @embedding_client.generate_embedding(query); semantic = search_semantic(..., embedding); keyword = Task.await(keyword_task). If full parallelism is not needed, sequential execution is acceptable since keyword search is sub-ms.
Embedding generation for query: uses the same `@embedding_client` compile-time DI as the worker. In tests, MockEmbeddingClient is already configured.

All three search functions (keyword, semantic, combined) should return the same response shape: `{:ok, %{results: [%{id, title, category, tags, relevance_score | similarity_score | final_score, snippet}], meta: %{total_count, limit, offset, ...}}}`.

Testing infrastructure: (1) All tests use async: true via DataCase/ConnCase, (2) Mox mocks defined in test/support/mocks.ex, (3) Default permissive stubs set in DataCase/ConnCase setup via stub_all_defaults/0 and Mox.set_mox_from_context(tags) for async isolation, (4) setup :verify_on_exit! in every test module, (5) Fixtures in test/support/fixtures.ex with build/2 (no DB) and fixture/2 (DB insert with auto-dependency resolution), (6) NEVER Application.put_env in tests — all service swapping via config/test.exs.
Config-based DI: `@embedding_client Application.compile_env(:loopctl, :embedding_client, Loopctl.Knowledge.EmbeddingClient)`. Same compile-time DI pattern as the Oban worker.

The @embedding_client implements Loopctl.Knowledge.EmbeddingBehaviour (defined in US-20.3). The real implementation uses Req for HTTP calls. In tests, use Mox.expect(Loopctl.MockEmbeddingClient, :generate_embedding, fn text -> {:ok, List.duplicate(0.1, 1536)} end).
